title: Kenyan police fire tear gas at protest against US Ebola quarantine centre plan
content: Protesters are concerned about cross-border infection risks and the lack of transparency from the government about the treatment centre.

originalTitle: Blow for Kenya's ex-deputy president as court upholds his impeachment
title: Blow for Kenya's ex-deputy president as court upholds his impeachment
content: The decision weakens Rigathi Gachagua's bid for the presidency, although his lawyers have vowed to challenge the ruling.

originalTitle: Somali referee Artan barred from entering US
title: Somali referee Artan barred from entering US
content: Omar Artan, who was set to be the first Somali to referee at the World Cup finals, is dropped from the list of officials after he was denied entry to the United States.

originalTitle: Hundred days of Israel’s latest war on Lebanon
title: Thinking of Our Friends: News from Far Away
content:
Hello, my lovely friends! It's your friendly reporter from Africa, here to share some news with you from our big, wide world. Today, we're going to talk about something happening far away, in places called Lebanon and Israel. Sometimes, countries can have big problems, just like sometimes friends have a disagreement. When these problems are very, very big, and people start fighting, grown-ups sometimes call it a 'war'. We hope that everyone can be friends and happy, always. This story is about hoping for peace and smiles for everyone.

What's Happening Far Away?

Imagine our Earth is like a giant playground with many, many different areas. Each area is a country, with its own special people and games. Right now, in two areas far from us, called Lebanon and Israel, there has been some big trouble. It’s like two neighbors having a very serious argument that has gone on for a long time. People living there are feeling very sad and worried because of the fighting. We want all children everywhere to feel safe and happy, right? Did you know? Lebanon and Israel are countries in a part of the world called the Middle East. They are not too far from Africa, but still a journey away! Just like your town has different streets, countries have different cities and villages.

A Long Time of Trouble

This big trouble has been going on for 100 days. Can you imagine? 100 days is a very, very long time! It's more than three whole months. Think about how many days you come to school – 100 days is like coming to school for half a year! When problems last this long, it makes many people very tired and sad. They just want the trouble to stop so they can go back to their normal lives, playing and learning. Did you know? Counting days helps us understand how long things last. It helps grown-ups keep track of when things started and when we hope they will end. We are all wishing for this trouble to end very soon.

Children and Families

When there is big trouble like this, children are often the ones who feel it the most. Imagine if you couldn't go to school every day, or if you couldn't play outside with your friends because it wasn't safe. Many children in Lebanon and Israel are going through this. They might feel scared, or they might have had to leave their homes to find a safer place. Their families are working hard to keep them safe and loved. Did you know? All children around the world, no matter where they live, love to play games, laugh, and learn new things! They all deserve to grow up in a safe and happy home. That's why we think about them and hope for peace.

Many, Many People

The news also tells us that sadly, many, many people have been very hurt, and some families have even lost their loved ones. When we say 'thousands', it's a very, very big number. Imagine if everyone in your school, and then everyone in ten more schools like yours, all got hurt or felt very sad because of fighting. That’s how big the number ‘thousands’ is. It means many, many lives have been touched by this trouble. We must remember that every person is important, and we want everyone to be safe. Did you know? One thousand is like having ten groups of 100. So, 'thousands' means a very, very big crowd of people. That's why it's so important to find ways for everyone to live together in peace, without fighting.

Why Is Peace So Important?

Peace is like a warm, sunny day where everyone feels happy and safe. It means no more fighting, no more worries, just smiles and friendship. When there is peace, children can go to school, play outside, and learn all the wonderful things the world has to offer. Families can stay together in their homes, and everyone can work and live happily. Peace makes the world a much brighter place for everyone. Did you know? When people talk and listen to each other, they can often find ways to solve problems without fighting. Like when you share your toys with a friend, everyone gets to play and be happy!

Our Wish for Friendship

So, my dear friends, even though this trouble is far away, we can all send our kind thoughts and wishes to the children and families in Lebanon and Israel. We can hope very, very much that the fighting stops soon, and that peace comes back to their homes. Let's imagine a world where all countries are friends, where children can play and learn, and where everyone feels safe and loved. Our wish for them is friendship, smiles, and sunshine every single day. Did you know? Even small wishes for peace from many people, like you, can make a big difference because it shows we care about each other, no matter where we live on this big, beautiful Earth!
summary: For 100 days, there has been big trouble and fighting between the countries of Lebanon and Israel, making many children and families very sad, and we hope for peace to return soon.
whyMatters: This is important because when countries have big problems and fight, many children and families get hurt or become very sad, and everyone deserves to live in a safe and happy world where people are kind to each other.

originalTitle: ‘Historic’ wave of Palestinian solidarity grows at universities in Germany
title: Students in Germany Are Showing They Care!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends! It's your friendly reporter, Pendo, here, bringing you news from far, far away. Today, we're going to talk about something really special happening in a country called Germany. Imagine big kids, even bigger than your older brothers or sisters, going to school. They are called university students, and they are doing something very kind.

Big Kids at Big Schools

Do you know what big schools are called? They are called universities! They are places where grown-up boys and girls go to learn even more amazing things after regular school. They learn about science, history, art, and so much more! It's like a big adventure for their brains. In Germany, many students go to these big universities. They are very smart, and they think a lot about the world. They learn about how things work, and they also learn about how people can help each other. **Did you know?** The oldest university in the world is said to be the University of Bologna in Italy! It started way back in the year 1088! That's a very, very long time ago, even before your great-great-grandparents were born. These students in Germany are doing something important. They are showing that they care about people who are far away. It's like when you draw a picture for someone who is sad to make them feel better.

Caring for Friends Far Away

Sometimes, people in different parts of the world have problems. Just like sometimes you might have a problem with a friend at school, or someone might be sad because their toy broke. When that happens, good friends want to help, right? They want to make things right and make everyone happy again. Well, the students in Germany feel like that. They know that some people far away, in a place called Palestine, are having a very hard time. They hear about it and they feel it in their hearts. They want to show them love and support, even though they are so far away. It's like when you see someone sad, and you give them a hug or ask if they are okay. These students are doing something like that, but in a grown-up way. They are showing what we call "solidarity." **Did you know?** "Solidarity" means standing together with others, especially when they need help or when things are difficult for them. It means saying, "We care about you! You are not alone!"

What Are They Asking For?

These big students are asking their universities to stop being "friends" or having special connections with some other big schools. These other schools are from a place called Israel. Imagine your school having a pen pal from another school. That's a kind of connection. The students in Germany are asking their universities to stop these special connections. They think that if their universities stop working with the schools from Israel, it might help make things better for the people in Palestine. They want peace. They want everyone to be safe and happy in their homes. It's like if two friends in the playground were arguing, and you asked them to stop playing together for a little while, hoping they might calm down and find a way to be kind again. The students believe this action will encourage fairness and help people who are struggling. **Did you know?** Germany is a country in Europe. It has many beautiful castles and yummy sausages! It's known for being a place where people like to think deeply and build amazing things.

Why is This Important?

This is important because it shows that even grown-ups, like university students, care a lot about being kind and fair to everyone in the world. They use their voices to ask for what they believe is right. They are not fighting or being mean. They are talking and asking peacefully for changes. They are teaching us that it's okay to stand up for people who are hurting. It reminds us that we are all part of one big global family, and we should always try to help each other. **Did you know?** There are over 2.5 million university students in Germany! That's a lot of smart people thinking and learning every day! These students want everyone to have a safe home and a happy life. They want all children to be able to play and learn without worry. It's a big wish, but it starts with people caring and speaking up.

What Can We Learn?

So, my little friends, what can we learn from the big students in Germany? We can learn that caring for others, even those far away, is very important. We can learn that using our voices, in a kind way, to ask for fairness can make a difference. Always try to be kind to your friends, listen to their problems, and remember that everyone, no matter where they live, deserves to be safe and happy. The students in Germany are showing us that by caring together, we can try to make the world a better place for everyone! That's all for today from your friendly reporter, Pendo! Keep spreading kindness!
summary: University students in Germany are asking their big schools to stop connections with other schools to show care and help people far away who are having a difficult time.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows that even big kids care about being kind and fair to everyone in the world, and they use their voices to ask for peace and help for others.

originalTitle: Russian attacks kill 5 in Ukraine as Zelenskyy hails talks with US envoys
title: Friends Talk for Peace in Ukraine!
content:
Hello, Friends!

Hi everyone! Your friendly news reporter from Africa is here. Today, we're talking about something important. It's about a country far away called Ukraine. Ukraine is a beautiful country. It has many lovely homes and schools. Just like where you live! But sometimes, big problems happen in the world.

A Big Problem Far Away

In Ukraine, there has been a big disagreement. Two countries have not been getting along. This has made some people in Ukraine very sad. Some people have even gotten hurt. It's like when friends have a big fight. Sometimes, it makes everyone feel bad. But just like friends, countries can also try to make up. **Did you know?** Ukraine is a country in Europe. It's very, very far from our homes in Africa. But even far away, people care about each other!

Leaders are Talking!

Here is some hopeful news! The president of Ukraine is a kind man named Mr. Zelenskyy. He wants to make things better. He has been talking to many important people. He talked to friends from a big country called the USA. He also talked to leaders from other countries in Europe. They all sat down to share their ideas. It was like a big meeting of helpers. Everyone wants to find a way for peace to come back. They want children in Ukraine to be safe. They want families to be happy. **Did you know?** A president is like the head leader of a country! They help make important decisions for everyone.

Why is Talking Important?

When people talk, they try to understand each other. This is called 'diplomacy'. It's a big word, but it just means talking to solve problems. Instead of fighting, grown-ups try to use their words. They try to find solutions that are good for everyone. This is a very smart way to make peace. President Zelenskyy said his talks were "very positive." This means they went well! It means he felt hopeful after talking to his friends. **Did you know?** Talking is always better than fighting! It helps us understand each other's feelings and find good solutions.

Who are the Helpers?

So, who are these friends helping Ukraine? We talked about Mr. Zelenskyy, the president of Ukraine. He is working very hard. Then there are leaders from the USA. They sent special helpers to talk with Mr. Zelenskyy. These helpers want to make peace happen too. And there are leaders from other countries in Europe. Europe is a big continent, just like Africa is a continent. Many European leaders also want to help Ukraine. They all wish for quiet and happiness in Ukraine. They want the children to go to school safely. They want everyone to live without fear. **Did you know?** Continents are very big pieces of land! Africa is a continent, and Europe is another continent.

What is Peace?

Peace means no fighting. It means everyone is safe and happy. It means children can play outside and go to school without worry. Peace means families can be together. It means people can work and build their homes. It is a wonderful feeling to live in peace. When leaders talk, they are trying to bring this wonderful feeling of peace back to Ukraine. They are working hard for it. **Did you know?** Many people all over the world pray and hope for peace every day. Peace makes the world a better place for everyone.

Hoping for a Brighter Tomorrow

The leaders will keep talking. They will keep working hard. They want to find the best way to make the disagreement stop. It takes time, like planting a seed and waiting for it to grow. But with effort and talking, good things can happen. We send our warmest wishes from Africa to the children and families in Ukraine. We hope for peace to return very, very soon. We are all part of one big global family. And families help each other.
summary: Important leaders are talking together to bring peace back to Ukraine, a country far away where people have been sad.
whyMatters: This is important because when leaders talk, they can stop problems and help everyone live safely and happily without fighting.

originalTitle: Trump tells BBC Netanyahu did not defy him
title: Leaders Are Friends! Mr. Trump Talks About His Friend Mr. Netanyahu
content:
Hello, Little Friends!

Hello, amazing friends! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you super important news from around our big, beautiful world. We live in Africa, and it's always good to know what's happening everywhere. Today, we're going to talk about leaders, just like your school principal or your community elder. These are people who help make big decisions for their countries.

Super Important People Talking!

Imagine you are talking to your best friend on the phone. You might be talking about your day, or a fun game, or maybe even a little problem. Well, grown-up leaders do this too! They talk to each other to help make things better for everyone in their countries. Talking helps them share ideas and work together.

Who Are These Leaders?

Today, we are talking about two very important leaders. One is Mr. Donald Trump. He used to be the President of a country called America. America is very far away, across the big ocean. The other leader is Mr. Benjamin Netanyahu. He is a leader from a country called Israel. These two leaders know each other very well. They have known each other for a long time. They have talked many times before. Did you know? The President of America is like the boss of a very big team! They help make decisions for millions and millions of people. It's a very big job, just like a head teacher for a giant school!

A News Lady Asked a Question!

A news lady from a place called BBC wanted to know something. The BBC is like a big news channel that tells people what's happening all over the world. This news lady, her name is Sarah, talked to Mr. Trump. She talked to him on the phone, just like you call your auntie or your grandmother. She asked him about his friend, Mr. Netanyahu. She also asked about some big, important talks happening in a place called Iran. When leaders have "talks," it means they are trying to solve problems and make sure everyone can live peacefully. It's like when you and your friends talk to sort out who gets to play with a toy first. Talking helps everyone understand each other better. It helps them find fair ways to share and be kind.

What Did Mr. Trump Say?

Mr. Trump said something very clear and very important. He said that Mr. Netanyahu did "not defy him." This is a big grown-up word, but it just means that Mr. Netanyahu listened to him. It means they were still working together. It means they were still good friends! Mr. Trump was happy that his friend listened. Imagine if you asked your friend to help you with something, and your friend listened and helped. That's what Mr. Trump was saying about Mr. Netanyahu. He said his friend was cooperative and understanding. They were on the same team, trying to make good choices for their people. They were trying to help each other solve big problems. Did you know? Leaders from different countries often talk on the phone or meet in person! This is called diplomacy. It's a fancy word for friends talking about important things. Just like when you visit your cousin in another village, they visit other leaders to talk!

Why Do Leaders Need to Be Friends?

It's really, really important for leaders to be friends and listen to each other. When leaders are friends, they can talk about big problems without getting angry. They can find solutions together. This helps to keep all the children, all the families, and all the communities in their countries safe and happy. No one wants to live in a place where leaders are fighting all the time. Think about your own friends. If you and your friends listen to each other, it's easier to play games and share toys, right? If you don't listen, there might be arguments and sadness. It's the same for big countries and their leaders! Friendly leaders make the world a better place for everyone. They can even share good ideas to help their people, like how to grow more food or build better schools.

Talking About Big Problems

The news lady also asked about important talks in a place called Iran. Sometimes, different countries have different ideas or problems they need to sort out. Leaders need to sit down and talk about these things very carefully. It's not about fighting or making things worse, but about finding a way to agree and live side by side peacefully. It's about being good neighbors. Mr. Trump was telling everyone that even when there are tough topics to discuss, he and Mr. Netanyahu were still communicating well. They were trying to work things out, like grown-ups do. This is a very good thing for everyone in the world. When leaders talk, they can stop small problems from becoming big ones. They can help protect families. Did you know? Countries have flags, just like your school might have a flag! These flags help us know which country a leader is from. Each flag tells a special story about that country!

Listening is Key!

So, the big message from Mr. Trump was that his friend, Mr. Netanyahu, listened to him. Listening is a super important skill for everyone, not just leaders. When you listen to your parents, your teachers, or your friends, you understand better. You can help better. You learn new things. And when leaders listen to each other, they can help make the world a safer, happier place for all of us. This means less worry for everyone, and more time for playing, learning, and growing strong! When leaders listen, they can build bridges, not walls.

What Does This Mean for Us?

Even though America and Israel are far, far away from our homes in Africa, it's good to know that leaders around the world are talking and trying to be friends. Because when leaders talk and listen, it helps to keep our big, beautiful world peaceful. It helps us all feel safe, no matter where we live. We hope they keep talking, keep listening, and keep working together for a bright future for all children, everywhere! Thank you for listening, little friends! See you next time for more amazing news from around our incredible world!
summary: Former American leader Mr. Trump told a news reporter that his friend, Mr. Netanyahu from Israel, listened to him when they talked about important world problems, showing they were still working together.
whyMatters: It's very important for leaders from different countries to talk and be friends, and to listen to each other, because it helps everyone live safely and peacefully in the whole wide world.
